decision-tree.root-question: Does the application need ≥2 retrieval modalities (e.g. SQL + vector, or vector + web)?

branch.question: Does the chosen LLM support reliable structured function calling?
Is web_search part of the registry?

branch.conclusion: Implement ToolUsingRAG with cap + log + allow-list. Apply r2-specific-descriptions, r3-cheap-routing-model, r4-intra-run-cache, r5-web-search-allowlist.
Block. Define and sign-off an allow-list before enabling web_search in any environment.
Implement ToolUsingRAG without web_search. Apply r1..r4.
Skip this methodology. Without reliable function calling, tool selection degrades to free-text parsing — use a single-store iterative agent instead.
Skip. Use a single-store RAG with iterative refinement; tool-selection overhead has no payoff when there is only one store.

example.p: Application has SQL (revenue table) + vector store (earnings call transcripts), Sonnet supports function calling, web_search not in registry → conclusion r1-cap-and-log → implement with 3-call cap.
